programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Steph McGovern present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Countryfile Summer Diaries
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor discovers how a secret wartime mission behind enemy lines brought one of our favourite seasonings, garlic, to the Isle of Wight. Keeley Donovan learns how a group of dedicated volunteers came to the dramatic rescue of a stranded sunburnt dolphin that was clinging to life(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell revisits a bungalow in Tipton, Dion Dublin meets father-and-daughter developers in Burnley and Martin Roberts examines the potential of three flats in Lewisham, south-east London(n)

programme.title: A Matter of Life and Debt
programme.desc: In St Ives, 24-year-old Tassy turns to the internet to crowd-fund a surf school, hoping to help local girls achieve their potential and increase their self-confidence(n)

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: Chris Bavin heads to Petworth in West Sussex to meet a group of residents entering Bloom for the first time who are hoping to get a disconnected community working together. These Bloomers want to engage the whole area, from the historic town centre to the modern housing estates on the outskirts, in an effort to bring the community spirit back to their neighbourhood(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp oversees proceedings as Tim Weeks and Richard Madley helps two teams look for items at the South of England Showground in Ardingly, West Sussex(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah is house hunting with a couple who have a budget of £650,000 and want to quit the city to find a house with great entertaining potential in the Cheshire countryside. Sonali also delves into the region's manufacturing heritage at one of the country's best preserved textile mills from the Industrial Revolution(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: After a successful time at Bredbury recycling centre, Sarah Moore hopes to generate some profit from two hidden gems. It's up to Josh and Oli to reimagine some discarded logs, while Sarah has to come up with a great idea to transform some old pipes into fabulous and desirable centerpieces(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein investigate the murder of a man in 1930s Lincolnshire. It was discovered that Arthur Major died as a result of strychnine poisoning and 42-year-old Ethel Major was arrested and charged with wilful murder. She protested her innocence and at trial was defended by a famous barrister, Norman Birkett KC, but the jury took just one hour to declare her guilty(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Anita Manning are at Culzean Castle in South Ayrshire, where they assess items including a selection of golf memorabilia and a trio of Moorcroft ceramics(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Nick Grimshaw and Emma Willis chat to celebrity guests and introduce stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich assist Emma and Steve, whose front garden in Hoveringham overlooking the River Trent, feels very much 'on display' to passers-by. The couple want greater want privacy without losing the precious river view and also wish to utilise a half-boat that they own in the design. Both Charlie and the siblings propose a similar clever idea to solve the privacy problem, a sunken courtyard garden. This only makes it harder for Emma and Steve to decide which design to pick(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mel makes the final preparations to leave Walford and as she covers her tracks at work, her behaviour makes Sharon suspicious. After one too many drinks, Mick takes Linda home and Stuart reassures him regarding Callum's whereabouts. As Robbie hopelessly flirts with Ash, Iqra kisses her in front of everyone(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: The second part of a two-legged edition of the quiz, which sees former England striker Darren Bent, 1993 and 1999 world champion 110m hurdler Colin Jackson, world Taekwondo title-winner Bianca Walkden and Olympic heptathlon medallist Kelly Sotherton join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker(n)

programme.title: Hold the Sunset
programme.desc: With her annoying sister Joan safely packed off home, Edith is thrilled when Phil announces that Jack Dugdale has made an offer on his house and he has accepted. Now they can get on and plan their future together, starting by setting a date for their wedding. However, it transpires that romance is in the air for more than just the one couple. Roger, Georgie and Bob attend a children's party, but are soon sent packing, while the family's portrayal in Wendy's book continues to cause angst. Comedy, starring Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins and John Cleese(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests Denise Lewis, Richard Osman, Robert Rinder and Katherine Ryan to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves(n)

programme.title: The King's Speech (2010)
programme.desc: The younger son of George V struggles to cope with an uncontrollable stammer, prompting his wife to enlist the aid of an eccentric Australian speech therapist. The support and friendship of the doctor prove invaluable when a crisis places the repressed prince on the throne, just as the outbreak of the Second World War leaves the country in need of a strong king. Oscar-winning fact-based drama, starring Colin Firth, Geoffrey Rush, Helena Bonham Carter and Guy Pearce(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The discovery of a Roman sword with traces of blood on it is linked to an unidentified, headless corpse found near Heathrow in March 2008. It leads the team members to a lock-up belonging to a bodybuilder called Mark Rix, who died of a suspected heart attack the same year, so they start looking for connections between the original murder and Rix's death. They are helped in their investigation by anthropologist Fiona Kennedy, for whom Dan begins to develop feelings - just as he is told his wife might be discharged from the secure hospital where she has spent the past several years. Tracy-Ann Obermann and Liz White guest star(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Steph McGovern present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Anna Jones and Gary O'Hanlon, and special guest Emily Atack. Plus, archive clips featuring Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nigella Lawson(n)

programme.title: Mary Berry's Quick Cooking
programme.desc: The food writer demonstrates recipes that deliver on flavour but save on time, inspired by the cuisine and cooking culture of places she visits. Mary beginning by making her first-ever visit to Rome to explore a cuisine that she has always adored, trying a new take on traditional pizza, uncovering a hidden gem in the Jewish quarter and cooking a speedy Roman classic with Michelin-starred chef Cristina Bowerman. Mary also whips up bruschetta, speedy pasta dishes and an irresistible tiramisu(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Arsenal v Tottenham Hotspur in the north London derby at the Emirates Stadium(n)

programme.title: Live Rowing
programme.desc: Matthew Pinsent presents live coverage from Ottensheim in Austria, where the world's best rowers have the dual aim of winning medals and securing their places at next summer's Olympics in Tokyo. An impressive 23 nations won medals at the recent World Cup regatta, so competition will be stiff. Britain topped the medals table at the 2016 Olympics with three golds but didn't win in any of the Olympic classes in Bulgaria last year, with Italy finishing first in the medals table for the second year in a row. Today's finals include the men's and women's fours and quadruple sculls. Race commentary comes from Garry Herbert and James Cracknell(n)

programme.title: Athletics
programme.desc: The Weltklasse Zurich. Action from the 13th and penultimate meeting of the Diamond League series, held at the Stadion Letzigrund. Triple European champion and member of Team GB Dina Asher-Smith competed over 200m, likely to be her last run over the distance prior to the World Championships in Doha. The stellar line-up also included the likes of world record holders Renaud Lavillenie in the pole vault, Barbora Spotakova in the javelin and steeplechaser Beatrice Chepkoech(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Avengers Assemble (2012)
programme.desc: A mystical artefact is stolen from a top-secret military base by Thor's brother Loki, who plans to use it to bring about an alien invasion. Spymaster Nick Fury brings together a team of superheroes, including Iron Man, Thor, Captain America and the Hulk, to defend Earth from the impending attack. Action adventure uniting the Marvel comic-book characters, starring Robert Downey Jr, Chris Evans, Chris Hemsworth, Mark Ruffalo, Scarlett Johansson, Tom Hiddleston, Jeremy Renner and Samuel L Jackson(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman invite another selection of famous faces to take part in the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. As always, the challengers are tasked with coming up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. This time, all the contestants come from the world of comedy, as Ralph Ineson, John Thomson, Janine Duvitski, Tony Maudsley, Melvyn Hayes, Stuart McGugan, Vicki Pepperdine and Rosie Cavaliero take part(n)

programme.title: Who Dares Wins
programme.desc: Game show in which two pairs of complete strangers team up, each battling to outdo the other by listing as much as they know about a given subject. The first couple to win two rounds get to play for £50,000 and each time the champions defeat a new set of opponents their winnings mount up(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Duffy volunteers at a care home and finds out how overlooked some residents can feel, and she decides to express her concerns to Charlie. Meanwhile, David is devastated when his ex-wife Rosalene calls him because their son, Ollie has run away. David finally finds him on a bridge, but Ollie slips off and is rushed into the emergency department where he undergoes a dangerous procedure. Elsewhere, Ethan pulls some strings to try and get Effie on the CF trial, and Archie returns for his first day back at work(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Burnley v Liverpool at Turf Moor and Southampton v Manchester United at St Mary's Stadium. Jurgen Klopp's men won both of their encounters with Sean Dyche's Clarets last season, including a 3-1 victory on this ground in December. The Reds had to come from behind in that fixture, with goals from James Milner, Roberto Firmino and Xherdan Shaqiri cancelling out Jack Cork's opener. Plus, action from Chelsea v Sheffield United at Stamford Bridge, Crystal Palace v Aston Villa at Selhurst Park, Leicester City v Bournemouth at the King Power Stadium, Manchester City v Brighton & Hove Albion at the Etihad Stadium, Newcastle United v Watford at St James' Park and West Ham United v Norwich City at London Stadium(n)

programme.title: The Rap Game UK
programme.desc: In the second episode, the artists are split into two groups and challenged to collaborate on a track together, blending their egos, styles and concerts(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: A look ahead to the opening round of regular-season fixtures, including Chicago Bears v Green Bay Packers and New England Patriots v Pittsburgh Steelers(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Ben Thompson and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Burnley v Liverpool at Turf Moor and Southampton v Manchester United at St Mary's Stadium. Jurgen Klopp's men won both of their encounters with Sean Dyche's Clarets last season, including a 3-1 victory on this ground in December. The Reds had to come from behind in that fixture, with goals from James Milner, Roberto Firmino and Xherdan Shaqiri cancelling out Jack Cork's opener. Plus, action from Chelsea v Sheffield United at Stamford Bridge, Crystal Palace v Aston Villa at Selhurst Park, Leicester City v Bournemouth at the King Power Stadium, Manchester City v Brighton & Hove Albion at the Etihad Stadium, Newcastle United v Watford at St James' Park and West Ham United v Norwich City at London Stadium(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break(n)

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Ria Hebden present topical debate covering religious, moral and ethical issues, with guest panellists and contributions from viewers(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned in the Kent seaside town of Margate, the Lancashire town of Carnforth, and a residence near Chesterfield(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones visits Burghley House near Stamford in Lincolnshire, discovering how Christianity is woven through its 450-year history and exploring some of its finest features(n)

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes(n)

programme.title: Hold the Sunset
programme.desc: With her annoying sister Joan safely packed off home, Edith is thrilled when Phil announces that Jack Dugdale has made an offer on his house and he has accepted. Now they can get on and plan their future together, starting by setting a date for their wedding. However, it transpires that romance is in the air for more than just the one couple. Roger, Georgie and Bob attend a children's party, but are soon sent packing, while the family's portrayal in Wendy's book continues to cause angst. Comedy, starring Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins and John Cleese(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a Co Durham couple hoping to move to North Yorkshire on a budget of £600,000, and also gets a masterclass in building dry stone walls(n)

programme.title: Shop Well for Less?
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Steph McGovern are in Milton Keynes to meet the Howarth family - one of the biggest spending families to ever feature on the programme - to help them change their shopping habits in an attempt to save money. A lack of time management means the family has slid into some bad shopping habits, often buying two or three of the same household items, and having recently renovated their home, the family find themselves with credit card debt. Can Alex and Steph get this family to rein in their spending and get on top of their debt?(n)

programme.title: Cinderella (2015)
programme.desc: An orphaned girl is treated as a servant by her cruel stepmother and her two daughters. Her life is changed for ever by the intervention of a kindly fairy godmother, who gives her the opportunity to attend a lavish royal ball and win the heart of a handsome prince. Romantic fantasy, starring Lily James, Cate Blanchett, Richard Madden and Helena Bonham Carter(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a Strictly Come Dancing version of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants Katya Jones, Joe McFadden, Debbie McGee, Giovanni Pernice, Ore Oduba, Oti Mabuse, Lesley Joseph and AJ Pritchard(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In a special edition marking 80 years since the first wave of evacuees were sent from towns and cities to the safety of the countryside, Steve Brown retraces his grandmother Rose's journey from Croydon to Cornwall. Sean Fletcher experiences life as an evacuee with schoolchildren at Acton Scott Historic Farm, while Margherita Taylor meets people who were relocated to Chatsworth House for safety during the war(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are at Morden Hall Park in South London, assessing items including a rare Cartier driver's watch, a self-portrait by John Lennon and Yoko Ono and a plate designed by Pablo Picasso. Jewellery specialist Joanna Hardy examines a diamond studded replica of a brooch originally designed for Wallis Simpson, while glass expert Andy McConnell inspects three early 18th-century pieces bought for just a few pence(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Tommy gathers everyone together to discuss recent tragic events and to give his orders, before the Shelby men travel to London to meet Mosley and it's soon revealed who has the upper hand. Linda heads to Arrow House to ask Lizzie's advice, Polly visits Aberama to pass on instructions from Tommy, who learns that one of the Peaky Blinders is in danger and sends Arthur and Johnny Dogs on an urgent mission to intervene. Period crime drama, starring Cillian Murphy, Helen McCrory and Sam Claflin(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, featuring Arsenal v Tottenham Hotspur at Emirates Stadium and Everton v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Goodison Park. The Gunners had not lost at home to their north London rivals since 2010 and won this fixture 4-2 last season, while Wolves were looking to emulate their 3-1 victory away to the Toffees last term. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's goals(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: The second part of a two-legged edition of the quiz, which sees former England striker Darren Bent, 1993 and 1999 world champion 110m hurdler Colin Jackson, world Taekwondo title-winner Bianca Walkden and Olympic heptathlon medallist Kelly Sotherton join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams look for items at the Royal Highland Centre in Ingliston, Edinburgh, with help from experts Roo Irvine and David Harper. Natasha Raskin Sharp keeps an eye on proceedings and also finds a very special portrait(n)

programme.title: A Matter of Life and Debt
programme.desc: In Cambridge, a £50,000 ethical loan helps turn an experiment into an enterprise for Kath, who uses natural beeswax to produce an alternative to cling film and plastic wrapping(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don makes a start on creating a wildflower patch. Adam Frost visits Sledmere Gardens in East Yorkshire where the borders have been planted with specific colour schemes in mind. The programme also catches up with a family in the Vale of Glamorgan whose members pack their small garden with colourful bedding plants for maximum impact(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's Time to Eat
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares a selection of stress-free recipes designed to help save time and calm hectic lives, beginning with dishes for when people are in a rush. She begins by demonstrating a trick for turning pancakes into a raspberry and peanut butter tray bake, while an omelette wrap filled with sun-dried tomato paste, olives and mushrooms is prepared in under 10 minutes. Nadiya also joins a shift at the largest mushroom producer in the country to find out what it takes to meet the demand for this fast, flexible and flavour-packed ingredient(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Miranda Krestovnikoff and Simon King join team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke on the panel show that tests wildlife knowledge, with Kate Humble asking the questions(n)

programme.title: Home Away from Home
programme.desc: House-proud couples from Buxton and Herefordshire swap homes to try out a new way of life, and see whether they enjoy the experience(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: Michael Buerk and the team resort to door knocking in their search for Dorothy Caines' heirs. Edward Burridge's history reveals a relative loved by celebrities(n)

programme.title: Emergency Rescue Down Under
programme.desc: Two British police officers hunt for criminals on the streets of Australia, and discover one of their suspects is another Brit. Plus, freak weather brings some bizarre casualties for an emergency vet from Bristol(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore how certain British ingredients make traditional combinations, preparing a pork and apple dish and a pear and Stilton tart. They also find out how Yorkshire pudding was made in the 1700s, and discover it was not originally served with beef(n)

programme.title: Tigers About the House
programme.desc: Spot and Stripe are outgrowing Giles Clark's home and developing adult behaviour, so the time comes for them to be reintegrated with the rest of the tigers at Australia Zoo. Their development is far from over, however, as they have their first swimming lesson and meet the adults they are going to share their lives with. But all is not well with Spot, who picks up a life-threatening eye condition(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Stephanie Connell and Charlie Ross browse for items of interest - and potential value - around Leicestershire, Northamptonshire and Rutland. Veteran auctioneer Charlie and dealer and Road Trip debutant Stephanie each hope to win out at the thrilling final auction in Market Harborough(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: TV doctors David Bull, Ellie Cannon, Linda Papadopoulos, Ranj Singh and Javid Abdelmoneim take on the general-knowledge champions to win a cash prize for charity. Jeremy Vine asks the questions(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On the trail of an historic achievement made at the dawn of the Edwardian era, Michael Portillo investigates the first radio signal to be sent across the Atlantic. Then, in Plymouth, he uncovers what happened to surviving crew members of the most famous ocean liner in history, the Titanic. Later, at Fowey, the former politician rediscovers a lost literary figure known as Q, who immortalised the town in his novels(n)

programme.title: Monkman & Seagull's Genius Guide to Britain
programme.desc: Eric and Bobby conclude their journey around the United Kingdom by heading to Scotland, where they visit the Edinburgh birthplace of 19th-century scientist James Clerk Maxwell, whose pioneering work includes the 1865 theory of electromagnetism. The duo then analyse the science behind the deep-fried Mars Bar before heading to Glasgow explore the Hunterian Museum's collection of antiquarian experimental instruments and delve into the personal correspondence of mathematical physicist and engineer Lord Kelvin(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites more contestants to brave the black chair, facing questions on specialised subjects including national flags, the life of Napoleon Bonaparte, CS Lewis's The Chronicles of Narnia and the life and career of tennis player Andre Agassi. They are then each afforded two minutes to demonstrate their prowess on general knowledge(n)

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer fly to Enniskillen, Co Fermanagh, to fish for pike, which eluded them in the previous series. They take to the waters of Lough Erne in their search for this mighty fish, guided by the very experienced Pascal. They discuss their respective bad habits and Bob gives Paul a thorough health update, before they retire to a local hostelry to sample traditional refreshments. Paul demonstrates how to perform life-saving CPR and Bob gives him a glimpse into his future by telling his fortune in an ancient Irish manner(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don adds some ornamental grasses to his borders for late summer impact and gives advice on how to look after pumpkins for a bountiful autumn harvest. Adam Frost discovers a colourful garden in Gloucestershire where hardy, tender and tropical plants jostle for space in both borders and containers, while Frances Tophill visits an allotment in north London where the main harvest is flowers for floral displays(n)

programme.title: Fosse/Verdon
programme.desc: The stress begins to get to Bob as he finishes the editing of Lenny, which has considerably overrun. Gwen prepares for the first day of the rehearsal for Chicago, where she has a cast-iron contract with the producers, with approval over almost every aspect of the production. As rehearsals begin Bob experiences chest pains and visits a hospital emergency room. Gwen arranges with a Broadway-loving doctor that Bob is given his own room, and assures him that Chicago won't proceed until he is better. True-life drama, starring Sam Rockwell and Michelle Williams(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig hosts an extended edition of the comedy quiz show, posing more perplexing questions to a selection of celebrity guests. As ever, the panellists will not necessarily be expected to deliver the correct answers, and Sandi will be handing out points to those who provide the 'most interesting' guesses. Challengers joining regular quizzer Alan Davies this week are Bill Bailey, Phill Jupitus and Claudia Winkleman(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Mark Urban presents analysis of the day's events(n)

programme.title: A Black and White Killing: The Case That Shook America
programme.desc: Part one of two. Mobeen Azhar investigates the murder of Larnell Bruce by a member of a white supremacist gang in Portland, and assesses the degree to which race played a role in the choice of victim. The investigation delves into the history of the seemingly liberal city, and uncovers a dark underbelly of racial tensions in the state of Oregon. The programme also examines the events of the trial, which were marked by clashes between anti-fascist groups and white supremacists on the streets. Featuring interviews with members of the militant groups and expert witnesses called on to testify(n)

programme.title: A Black and White Killing: The Case That Shook America
programme.desc: Part two of two. Mobeen Azhar seeks to understand the complications surrounding the lives and behaviour of both perpetrator Russell Courtier and victim Larnell Bruce, looking more closely into the nature and extent of European Kindred's influence on the wider community. He investigates the brainwashing effect that membership of Oregon's racist prison gangs can have on young men, before hearing the jury deliver its verdict on whether Courtier is a racist murderer(n)

programme.title: The Chefs' Brigade
programme.desc: Jason Atherton takes 10 chefs from pubs, cafes and caterers across the UK on a culinary journey across Europe, and gives them a crash course in preparing food fit for the finest restaurants. The first destination is Puglia in southern Italy, where food is prepared on the principle of making the most of what is to hand. The chefs are tested on their pasta-making skills, and pitted in a culinary contest against a well-regarded local restaurant. However, Jason faces a tough choice of whether to send a struggling member of the group home and fly in a replacement(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Iain is joined by Steve Bugeja, Lauren Pattison, Matthew Crosby and Thanyia Moore(n)

programme.title: Ali-A's Superchargers
programme.desc: The vlogger helps a brother and sister duo from Liverpool turn their family people-carrier from a boring wagon into a head-turning motor(n)

programme.title: Mustangs FC
programme.desc: The Mustangs need to get serious before the season officially starts(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Long-running children's magazine programme(n)

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Kate Humble hosts the panel show that tests the wildlife knowledge of captains Chris Packham, Lucy Cooke and guests Steve Backshall and Ajay Tegala in a variety of rounds(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60 on a Mission
programme.desc: Steve Backshall embarks on another global trek, placing himself in the path of some of the world's most dangerous animals. His journey begins in the Arctic, where he is stalked by a huge male polar bear, and gains the attention of a herd of boisterous walruses. He also encounters head-banging muskoxen and a ghostly white whale(n)

programme.title: The Animal's Guide to Britain
programme.desc: Chris Packham explores the lives of grassland creatures, observing the eating habits of starlings, whose food supplies are dwindling due to modern pesticides. Plus, the latest research into how honeybees collect nectar and pollen, and the ways in which horseshoe bats, brown hares and barn owls are learning to adapt to modern Britain(n)

programme.title: The Princess and the Frog (2009)
programme.desc: A hard-working New Orleans waitress meets an arrogant prince who has been turned into a frog by a magic spell. When she tries to break the enchantment by kissing him, she also ends up transformed into an amphibian, and together they go in search of the voodoo queen who can change them back. Disney animated musical fantasy, with the voices of Anika Noni Rose and Bruno Campos(n)

programme.title: Nigel Slater: Eating Together
programme.desc: Nigel visits three home cooks to learn about well-loved variations on custard, including a fragrant Iraqi dish that takes Linda back to happy days in Baghdad. His tour of the flavours of modern Britain reveals the secrets behind home-baked Portuguese custard tarts and a delicious Malaysian custard that is not yellow, but green. Finally, Nigel comes up with some extravagant profiteroles that combine a touch of all these styles(n)

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: Falklands War veteran and charity campaigner Simon Weston takes Brian Turner back to his childhood home of Nelson, in Caerphilly, where the chef prepares two dishes from his youth(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction(n)

programme.title: Home Front Heroes
programme.desc: Five famous faces reflect on the wartime experiences of close family members, discovering just how significant their contributions were. In the first edition actress Sue Johnston follows in the footsteps of her Auntie May, who was a nurse in Merseyside during the Second Word War(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Sylvia Syms revisits some of the great British war films that helped boost the nation's morale in the difficult years that followed the end of the Second World War. Among the silver screen gems highlighted are In Which We Serve, Reach for the Sky, Bridge on the River Kwai and The Dam Busters. The programme includes archive interviews with stars including Noel Coward, Richard Attenborough, David Lean, Alec Guinness, Kenneth More and John Mills, and Sylvia shares her memories of filming her own contribution to the genre, Ice Cold in Alex(n)

programme.title: The Man Who Never Was (1956)
programme.desc: Allied forces prepare to invade Europe through Sicily - but to throw their adversaries off their scent, they devise an ingenious and complicated plan to make the enemy believe the invasion will happen somewhere else. Fact-based Second World War drama, starring Clifton Webb, Gloria Grahame, Stephen Boyd, Robert Flemyng and Andre Morell(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: RNLI volunteers in Pembrokeshire, including brothers Hal, Will and Ben, race to the scene when a plane makes an emergency crash landing on one of Wales' busiest beaches. In Jersey, the St Catherine crew are pushed to the brink when they search for a man who has gone missing at sea in the middle of a fierce winter storm. On the Wirral, the crew of Hoylake launch their specialist rescue hovercraft and race the incoming tide to try and save a horse, which has become stuck fast in the mud and at risk from drowning(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Road to Mexico
programme.desc: For Mexicans, their most revered dishes come from the south, and their Mecca is Oaxaca, where locals still dress in traditional costume. Rick explores the home of Mexico's national cheese, Queso Oaxacana. It's also a land where vanilla grows wild and cacao orchards are harvested to make superb chocolate(n)

programme.title: Proms Encore
programme.desc: Katie Derham is joined on the bandstand by legendary jazz pianist Monty Alexander, conductor Ilan Volkov and music historian Dr Hannah French for a special edition celebrating the 150th birthday of Proms co-founder Henry Wood. YolanDa Brown explores Wood's legacy and Tom Service takes another maestro for a musical chat in an Austin Maestro - this time it's Heritage Orchestra chief conductor Jules Buckley(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: The Eastgate branch of the bank finds itself in urgent need of a new manager - and who should step into the breach but Sergeant Wilson. His new life does not last long, however, as his place of work is bombed the day he arrives. Comedy first shown in 1970, starring John Le Mesurier(n)

programme.title: Wild Shetland - Scotland's Viking Frontier
programme.desc: Ewan McGregor narrates a portrait of wildlife on the Shetland Islands, tracing the course of a breeding season from the depths of winter to high summer. Cameras follow puffins, diving gannets and storm petrels nesting in an ancient Pictish tower, a mother otter protecting her young twin cubs, and capture a pod of orcas hunting seals of the island's rocky coasts(n)

programme.title: Their Finest (2016)
programme.desc: Former secretary Catrin Cole, newly appointed by the British Ministry of Information as a scriptwriter for propaganda films, joins the cast and crew of a major production while the Blitz rages around them. Comedy drama, starring Gemma Arterton, Sam Claflin, Richard E Grant and Bill Nighy(n)

programme.title: Southside with You (2016)
programme.desc: Biographical drama chronicling the afternoon in the summer of 1989, when the future 44th President of the United States Barack Obama and Michelle Robinson went on their first date across the South Side of Chicago. Starring Tika Sumpter and Parker Sawyers(n)

programme.title: Is Anybody There? (2008)
programme.desc: A lonely 10-year-old boy lives in a building his parents have converted into an old people's retirement home. He initially resents having to share his life with the elderly residents, but when a retired magician comes to stay he strikes up an unlikely friendship and finds his life transformed. Drama, starring Michael Caine, Bill Milner, Anne-Marie Duff, David Morrissey and Leslie Phillips(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter U, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don adds some ornamental grasses to his borders for late summer impact and gives advice on how to look after pumpkins for a bountiful autumn harvest. Adam Frost discovers a colourful garden in Gloucestershire where hardy, tender and tropical plants jostle for space in both borders and containers, while Frances Tophill visits an allotment in north London where the main harvest is flowers for floral displays(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker heads to Warwickshire to marks the centenary of the death of Joseph Arch, a farm labourer who helped form the National Agricultural Labourers' Union in 1872 and later became an MP. Elsewhere, Adam Henson battles against the elements to get his harvest safely home, and John Craven, Cerys Matthews and Simon King reveal the final 12 pictures the viewers will get to vote on in this year's Countryfile photographic competition.(n)

programme.title: The Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Carole Baxter travels to Little Loch Broom in Wester Ross to visit the garden of Sue Pomeroy and Will Soos, who have created a coastal garden at the end of the world(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's Summer Feasts
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain prepares an array of mouth-watering dishes that she will serve up to her loved ones at her own summer party feast, including a barbecued butterflied leg of lamb with sticky rhubarb glaze. She makes side dishes of kiwi and feta salad and edamame wild rice, as well as olive and rosemary bread shaped like a crown. For dessert, she goes all out with a magical princess torte cake and an ingenious raspberry ripple eclair, and creates a thirst-quenching drink in the form of an elderflower cooler(n)

programme.title: Top of the Shop with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: In the penultimate heat, four producers of drinks compete against each other for a place in the final, as they test their products out on locals in Malhamdale in the Yorkshire Dales. Amy and Dan offer up their cold brew coffee, while Barbara trials her raspberry and rose cordial. Elizabeth and Shirley brew and sell a fermented tea, and Isatou and Haddy offer up a baobab smoothie(n)

programme.title: Live Rowing
programme.desc: Matthew Pinsent presents live coverage from Ottensheim in Austria, where the annual event concludes. The men's and women's eights take centre stage today, and Great Britain's men are expected to battle it out with reigning champions Germany for the title. With commentary by Garry Herbert and James Cracknell(n)

programme.title: Triathlon
programme.desc: Louise Minchin presents action from the Grand Final, as the final overall standings in the men's and women's competitions were confirmed(n)

programme.title: Equestrian
programme.desc: Action from Lumuhlen in Germany, where Great Britain entered as the defending team champions, but were expected to face stiff competition from the hosts(n)

programme.title: The Ganges with Sue Perkins
programme.desc: The comedian finds herself halfway down the mighty river in the ancient city of Varanasi. Many travellers to India stop off in this extraordinary place to take in the sights for a day or two. Sue explores the life of its people, fishing with the city's young men, trying handloom weaving and meeting an all-female vigilante group. But as she discovers, the city has a unique significance for India's 950 million Hindus. For them, the main reason to come here is to die(n)

programme.title: Grizzly Bear Cubs and Me
programme.desc: Part one of two. Gordon Buchanan joins the Pazhetnovs, a pioneering family of biologists in Russia as they attempt to rehab grizzly bear cub orphans. There are more than 100,000 grizzlies in Russia and as humans encroach further into this vast wilderness, conflict is increasing. The Pazhetnovs have been rescuing orphaned cubs for nearly three decades and have successfully returned more than 200 of them back to the wild. Gordon arrives in February when six cubs have just been rescued. They are just five weeks old and each weigh less than a bag of sugar(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Former Dragon Theo Paphitis returns to the panel to stand in for Touker Suleyman, joining Sara Davies, Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden and Tej Lalvani in assessing more business ideas. This week's entrepreneurs pitch their ideas for a diet app, a clothes-mending workshop, a gadget for repairing bicycle chains, and a range of dog treats(n)

programme.title: Untouchable: The Rise and Fall of Harvey Weinstein
programme.desc: The story of how the film producer acquired and deployed his power, charting how he allegedly abused women with seeming impunity throughout his career - from his early days as a music promoter to running movie company Miramax with his brother Bob. Women who came forward to accuse Weinstein of sexual harassment and assault tell their stories in detail, including Rosanna Arquette, Erika Rosenbaum and Paz De La Huerta, while the reporters who broke the story detail his ruthless attempts to preserve power, even as scandal threatened to engulf him(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Direct from the stage at London's Hammersmith Apollo, Channel 4's Chatty Man host Alan Carr is joined by comedy circuit stars Francesca Martinez and Nish Kumar for another celebration of stand-up comedy(n)

programme.title: Life of Crime (2013)
programme.desc: Two criminals kidnap the wife of a corrupt real-estate tycoon and demand a $1million ransom. However, the businessman is enjoying a holiday in the Bahamas with his mistress and does not want his wife back, setting in motion a complex series of double-crosses. Comedy based on Elmore Leonard's novel The Switch, starring Jennifer Aniston, Mos Def, Isla Fisher and Tim Robbins(n)

programme.title: World News Today
programme.desc: The day's leading stories(n)

programme.title: BBC Proms 2019
programme.desc: Katie Derham presents flavours of Finland, Russia and Germany from three 20th-century favourites. Yannick Nézet-Séguin and the Bavarian Radio Symphony Orchestra perform Sibelius's turbulent first Symphony, Prokofiev's second Violin Concerto - performed by Gil Shaham - and the orchestral suite from Richard Strauss's blockbuster opera, Der Rosenkavalier(n)

programme.title: Smashing Hits! The 80s Pop Map of Britain and Ireland
programme.desc: Midge Ure and Kim Appleby explore the sounds that came from Scotland, Ireland and Wales. They start in Glasgow with the American influences that shaped a substantial part of Scottish music, look at the punk and folk backdrop to Irish music and, finally, delve into the Welsh merger of folk and punk. The programme features evocative archive, music and interviews with significant figures of the era including Bob Geldof, Clare Grogan from Altered Images, Pat Kane from Hue and Cry, Moya Brennan of Clannad and Mike Peters from legendary Welsh band the Alarm(n)

programme.title: BBC Proms 2019
programme.desc: Clara Amfo introduces a prom celebrating jazz and blues legend Nina Simone. The Metropole Orkest is led by Jules Buckley, joined by Ledisi, Lisa Fischer and other singers in this tribute to the acclaimed singer, songwriter and political activist. The titles include standards such as Feeling Good, My Baby Just Cares for Me and I Put a Spell on You(n)

programme.title: Hits, Hype & Hustle: An Insider's Guide to the Music Business
programme.desc: Music public relations expert Alan Edwards tells the story of why so many bands are getting back together, what happens when they do - and how such reunions have changed the music business. He starts by recalling the UK's first revival concert, which took place over 40 years ago at Wembley Stadium. Featuring Bill Hailey and the Comets, Little Richard and Jerry Lee Lewis - the event opened the eyes of promoters to the power of yesterday's hit-makers to reach an audience and make money. From there, Alan takes on a musical journey through some of the biggest reunions of the last 30 years. Contributors include Glen Matlock, Debbie Harry, Stewart Copeland and Melanie C(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Revisiting the year when Simply Red, the Housemartins, Sinitta, the Communards and Swing Out Sister hit the charts(n)

programme.title: Smashing Hits! The 80s Pop Map of Britain and Ireland
programme.desc: Midge Ure and Kim Appleby explore the sounds that came from Scotland, Ireland and Wales. They start in Glasgow with the American influences that shaped a substantial part of Scottish music, look at the punk and folk backdrop to Irish music and, finally, delve into the Welsh merger of folk and punk. The programme features evocative archive, music and interviews with significant figures of the era including Bob Geldof, Clare Grogan from Altered Images, Pat Kane from Hue and Cry, Moya Brennan of Clannad and Mike Peters from legendary Welsh band the Alarm(n)

programme.title: Britain's Secret Seas
programme.desc: Explorer Paul Rose teams up with biologist Tooni Mahto and underwater archaeologist Frank Pope to explore the secrets of the waters surrounding Britain. The trio begin by highlighting marine life in and around the western seas, including basking sharks off the Cornish shore and spider crabs in the shallows of south Wales, as well as creatures in a protected area around Lundy Island off the Devon coast(n)

programme.title: The Private Life of Chickens
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty learns about the origins, social behaviour and intelligence of chickens. He discovers how the pecking order is determined, and carries out a series of experiments to test their abilities, including their capacity for coping with predators and whether they can distinguish human symbols(n)

programme.title: Darkness: Those Who Kill
programme.desc: While investigating the disappearance of a young woman from a Copenhagen suburb, Jan Michelsen learns of a similar case from the same neighbourhood 10 years previously. When he discovers human remains in a lake he suspects a link between the two crimes and brings in an expert on serial killers. Danish crime drama, starring Kenneth M Christensen(n)

programme.title: Darkness: Those Who Kill
programme.desc: Jan and Louise's investigation into Natasha's death leads them to Anders Kjeldsen, a man who has been arrested before for violent crimes - including rape. Danish crime drama, starring Kenneth M Christensen(n)

programme.title: The Vietnam War
programme.desc: Richard Nixon wins the presidency, promising law and order at home and peace overseas, while soldiers on both sides witness terrible savagery and unflinching courage in Vietnam(n)

programme.title: The Vietnam War
programme.desc: President Nixon begins withdrawing American troops, news breaks of a massacre carried out by US soldiers and an incursion into Cambodia reignites anti-war protests(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988

programme.title: Popular Voices at the BBC
programme.desc: A celebration of bravura singers who brought the house down in front of BBC cameras with their showstopping vocal delivery and performances in such programmes as How It is, Dusty, Top of the Pops, It's Lulu and Wogan. Clips range from Mahalia Jackson's breath-taking gospel delivery to Ella Fitzgerald's rapid-fire scat, and Tom Jones and Dusty Springfield's '60s big balladry to the soul divas of the '80s and beyond. Plus, vocal eccentrics including Kate Bush and Bjork and the staggering vocal range of Jeff Buckley, captured in his only BBC performance on The Late Show in 1994(n)

programme.title: Britain's Secret Seas
programme.desc: Explorer Paul Rose teams up with biologist Tooni Mahto and underwater archaeologist Frank Pope to explore the secrets of the waters surrounding Britain. programme.desc: Tom Service and Kwame Ryan introduce a 150th anniversary tribute to Henry Wood, founder-conductor of the Proms. It features many of the works he premiered and arranged across almost 50 years of the festival, and reflects his wide musical tastes, from Wagner and John Ireland to Ravel and Percy Grainger(n)

programme.title: Arena
programme.desc: Japanese contemporary artist Yayoi Kusama's work pushed boundaries that often alienated her from her peers and those in power in the art world. Heather Lenz's documentary profiles her work, including the much-visited Infinity Mirror Room installations, which defies classification(n)

programme.title: Ryan Gander: The Idea of Japan
programme.desc: The artist explores Japanese visual culture through symbols, including the geisha, samurai and cherry blossom to find out what they reveal about their past, present and future(n)

programme.title: Strangeways - Britain's Toughest Prison Riot
programme.desc: Twenty-five years after the biggest riot in British penal history, this documentary brings together the ringleaders of the trouble and the prison guards they battled during the course of three weeks of anarchy - which brought HMP Strangeways to its knees. Former inmates of the Manchester facility describe the explosion of violence that erupted on April 1, 1990, when 1,600 of them escaped from their cells and ran amok, while prison officers recall fearing for their lives as they were driven out of the building, leaving the rioters inside to settle scores and hunt down sex offenders(n)

programme.desc: Everyone's surprised to hear the Great Wizard is making an unexpected visit to Cackle's Academy. He's had a tip off that the school is going rapidly downhill and it looks like non-magical Mildred is at the heart of the problems. Miss Cackle (really Agatha) refuses to expel Mildred and waxes lyrical about her. The Great Wizard insists that Mildred should perform a magical display to prove herself. programme.desc: With just 2 years' experience, Scottish teenager Iona has only five months to prepare for the biggest international competition of her life, the World Junior Surf Championships in the Azores, Portugal. It's a race against time as she trains in sub-zero conditions and battles the unpredictable seas of her home shores to find the perfect wave. It's punishing but day after day Iona dons her wetsuit and dives in. But surfing off Scotland's coast is a world away from the warm, volcanic waters of the Azores, where Iona will be up against the gnarliest (treacherous) waves she has ever faced as well as some of the most experienced and fearless surfers on the planet. Determined not to let herself or her country down, will Iona master the surf - and the pressure - to come home a champion?(n)

Keeley Donovan learns how a group of dedicated volunteers came to the dramatic rescue of a stranded sunburnt dolphin that was clinging to life(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.desc: Chris Bavin heads to Petworth in West Sussex to meet a group of residents entering Bloom for the first time who are hoping to get a disconnected community working together. These Bloomers want to engage the whole area, from the historic town centre to the modern housing estates on the outskirts, in an effort to bring the community spirit back to their neighbourhood(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

Sonali also delves into the region's manufacturing heritage at one of the country's best preserved textile mills from the Industrial Revolution(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: After a successful time at Bredbury recycling centre, Sarah Moore hopes to generate some profit from two hidden gems. It's up to Josh and Oli to reimagine some discarded logs, while Sarah has to come up with a great idea to transform some old pipes into fabulous and desirable centerpieces(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein investigate the murder of a man in 1930s Lincolnshire. It was discovered that Arthur Major died as a result of strychnine poisoning and 42-year-old Ethel Major was arrested and charged with wilful murder. She protested her innocence and at trial was defended by a famous barrister, Norman Birkett KC, but the jury took just one hour to declare her guilty(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Anita Manning are at Culzean Castle in South Ayrshire, where they assess items including a selection of golf memorabilia and a trio of Moorcroft ceramics(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Nick Grimshaw and Emma Willis chat to celebrity guests and introduce stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Kate Humble: Off the Beaten Track
programme.desc: In mid-Wales, Kate and sheepdog Teg have a taste of stone-age life mucking in with a farmer and an archaeologist who are building a medieval-type settlement. Next, they explore the potential death traps of historical legacies at an old lead mine, where the South and Mid Wales Cave Rescue Team practise their skills using Teg as the damsel in distress. Kate also learns about the plight of Welsh mountain ponies, whose numbers have dropped dramatically, and discovers whether her legs are built for mountain biking(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mel makes the final preparations to leave Walford and as she covers her tracks at work, her behaviour makes Sharon suspicious. After one too many drinks, Mick takes Linda home and Stuart reassures him regarding Callum's whereabouts. As Robbie hopelessly flirts with Ash, Iqra kisses her in front of everyone(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: The second part of a two-legged edition of the quiz, which sees former England striker Darren Bent, 1993 and 1999 world champion 110m hurdler Colin Jackson, world Taekwondo title-winner Bianca Walkden and Olympic heptathlon medallist Kelly Sotherton join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker(n)

programme.title: Hold the Sunset
programme.desc: With her annoying sister Joan safely packed off home, Edith is thrilled when Phil announces that Jack Dugdale has made an offer on his house and he has accepted. Now they can get on and plan their future together, starting by setting a date for their wedding. However, it transpires that romance is in the air for more than just the one couple. Roger, Georgie and Bob attend a children's party, but are soon sent packing, while the family's portrayal in Wendy's book continues to cause angst. Comedy, starring Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins and John Cleese(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests Denise Lewis, Richard Osman, Robert Rinder and Katherine Ryan to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves(n)

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen references clips from half a century and more of Welsh television as he sets out to discover the truth about work. It's a story of bouncers, bin men and female wrestlers, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past(n)

programme.title: The King's Speech (2010)
programme.desc: The younger son of George V struggles to cope with an uncontrollable stammer, prompting his wife to enlist the aid of an eccentric Australian speech therapist. The support and friendship of the doctor prove invaluable when a crisis places the repressed prince on the throne, just as the outbreak of the Second World War leaves the country in need of a strong king. Oscar-winning fact-based drama, starring Colin Firth, Geoffrey Rush, Helena Bonham Carter and Guy Pearce(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The discovery of a Roman sword with traces of blood on it is linked to an unidentified, headless corpse found near Heathrow in March 2008. It leads the team members to a lock-up belonging to a bodybuilder called Mark Rix, who died of a suspected heart attack the same year, so they start looking for connections between the original murder and Rix's death. They are helped in their investigation by anthropologist Fiona Kennedy, for whom Dan begins to develop feelings - just as he is told his wife might be discharged from the secure hospital where she has spent the past several years. Tracy-Ann Obermann and Liz White guest star(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Steph McGovern present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Anna Jones and Gary O'Hanlon, and special guest Emily Atack. Plus, archive clips featuring Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nigella Lawson(n)

programme.title: Mary Berry's Quick Cooking
programme.desc: The food writer demonstrates recipes that deliver on flavour but save on time, inspired by the cuisine and cooking culture of places she visits. Mary beginning by making her first-ever visit to Rome to explore a cuisine that she has always adored, trying a new take on traditional pizza, uncovering a hidden gem in the Jewish quarter and cooking a speedy Roman classic with Michelin-starred chef Cristina Bowerman. Mary also whips up bruschetta, speedy pasta dishes and an irresistible tiramisu(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Arsenal v Tottenham Hotspur in the north London derby at the Emirates Stadium(n)

programme.title: Live Rowing
programme.desc: Matthew Pinsent presents live coverage from Ottensheim in Austria, where the world's best rowers have the dual aim of winning medals and securing their places at next summer's Olympics in Tokyo. An impressive 23 nations won medals at the recent World Cup regatta, so competition will be stiff. Britain topped the medals table at the 2016 Olympics with three golds but didn't win in any of the Olympic classes in Bulgaria last year, with Italy finishing first in the medals table for the second year in a row. Today's finals include the men's and women's fours and quadruple sculls. Race commentary comes from Garry Herbert and James Cracknell(n)

programme.title: Athletics
programme.desc: The Weltklasse Zurich. Action from the 13th and penultimate meeting of the Diamond League series, held at the Stadion Letzigrund. Triple European champion and member of Team GB Dina Asher-Smith competed over 200m, likely to be her last run over the distance prior to the World Championships in Doha. The stellar line-up also included the likes of world record holders Renaud Lavillenie in the pole vault, Barbora Spotakova in the javelin and steeplechaser Beatrice Chepkoech(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Avengers Assemble (2012)
programme.desc: A mystical artefact is stolen from a top-secret military base by Thor's brother Loki, who plans to use it to bring about an alien invasion. Spymaster Nick Fury brings together a team of superheroes, including Iron Man, Thor, Captain America and the Hulk, to defend Earth from the impending attack. Action adventure uniting the Marvel comic-book characters, starring Robert Downey Jr, Chris Evans, Chris Hemsworth, Mark Ruffalo, Scarlett Johansson, Tom Hiddleston, Jeremy Renner and Samuel L Jackson(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman invite another selection of famous faces to take part in the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. As always, the challengers are tasked with coming up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. This time, all the contestants come from the world of comedy, as Ralph Ineson, John Thomson, Janine Duvitski, Tony Maudsley, Melvyn Hayes, Stuart McGugan, Vicki Pepperdine and Rosie Cavaliero take part(n)

programme.title: Who Dares Wins

The first couple to win two rounds get to play for £50,000 and each time the champions defeat a new set of opponents their winnings mount up(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Duffy volunteers at a care home and finds out how overlooked some residents can feel, and she decides to express her concerns to Charlie. Meanwhile, David is devastated when his ex-wife Rosalene calls him because their son, Ollie has run away. David finally finds him on a bridge, but Ollie slips off and is rushed into the emergency department where he undergoes a dangerous procedure. Elsewhere, Ethan pulls some strings to try and get Effie on the CF trial, and Archie returns for his first day back at work(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Burnley v Liverpool at Turf Moor and Southampton v Manchester United at St Mary's Stadium. Jurgen Klopp's men won both of their encounters with Sean Dyche's Clarets last season, including a 3-1 victory on this ground in December. The Reds had to come from behind in that fixture, with goals from James Milner, Roberto Firmino and Xherdan Shaqiri cancelling out Jack Cork's opener. Plus, action from Chelsea v Sheffield United at Stamford Bridge, Crystal Palace v Aston Villa at Selhurst Park, Leicester City v Bournemouth at the King Power Stadium, Manchester City v Brighton & Hove Albion at the Etihad Stadium, Newcastle United v Watford at St James' Park and West Ham United v Norwich City at London Stadium(n)

programme.title: The Rap Game UK
programme.desc: In the second episode, the artists are split into two groups and challenged to collaborate on a track together, blending their egos, styles and concerts(n)

Emotions run high when the sheriffs visit an Italian car mechanic who owes money in a dispute over a van repair(n)

programme.title: Sam & Shauna's Big Cook Out
programme.desc: Samantha Evans and Shauna Guinn travel to the small town of Deri near Caerphilly to celebrate the success of its rugby club, which has gone from the brink of extinction just four years ago to being crowned league champions. To mark the achievements of the players and the volunteers who run the club, Sam and Shauna cook up a huge venison feast with their help(n)

programme.title: Kate Humble: Off the Beaten Track
programme.desc: In mid-Wales, Kate and sheepdog Teg have a taste of stone-age life mucking in with a farmer and an archaeologist who are building a medieval-type settlement.
