Kate also learns about the plight of Welsh mountain ponies, whose numbers have dropped dramatically, and discovers whether her legs are built for mountain biking(n)

programme.title: Cinderella (2015)
programme.desc: An orphaned girl is treated as a servant by her cruel stepmother and her two daughters. Her life is changed for ever by the intervention of a kindly fairy godmother, who gives her the opportunity to attend a lavish royal ball and win the heart of a handsome prince. Romantic fantasy, starring Lily James, Cate Blanchett, Richard Madden and Helena Bonham Carter(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a Strictly Come Dancing version of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants Katya Jones, Joe McFadden, Debbie McGee, Giovanni Pernice, Ore Oduba, Oti Mabuse, Lesley Joseph and AJ Pritchard(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In a special edition marking 80 years since the first wave of evacuees were sent from towns and cities to the safety of the countryside, Steve Brown retraces his grandmother Rose's journey from Croydon to Cornwall. Sean Fletcher experiences life as an evacuee with schoolchildren at Acton Scott Historic Farm, while Margherita Taylor meets people who were relocated to Chatsworth House for safety during the war(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are at Morden Hall Park in South London, assessing items including a rare Cartier driver's watch, a self-portrait by John Lennon and Yoko Ono and a plate designed by Pablo Picasso. Jewellery specialist Joanna Hardy examines a diamond studded replica of a brooch originally designed for Wallis Simpson, while glass expert Andy McConnell inspects three early 18th-century pieces bought for just a few pence(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Tommy gathers everyone together to discuss recent tragic events and to give his orders, before the Shelby men travel to London to meet Mosley and it's soon revealed who has the upper hand. Linda heads to Arrow House to ask Lizzie's advice, Polly visits Aberama to pass on instructions from Tommy, who learns that one of the Peaky Blinders is in danger and sends Arthur and Johnny Dogs on an urgent mission to intervene. Period crime drama, starring Cillian Murphy, Helen McCrory and Sam Claflin(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, featuring Arsenal v Tottenham Hotspur at Emirates Stadium and Everton v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Goodison Park. The Gunners had not lost at home to their north London rivals since 2010 and won this fixture 4-2 last season, while Wolves were looking to emulate their 3-1 victory away to the Toffees last term. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's goals(n)

programme.title: Mavericks: Sport's Lost Heroes
programme.desc: A profile of 1970s Formula One driver Tom Pryce, who showed great promise but died in tragic circumstances at the 1977 South African Grand Prix(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Steph McGovern present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Countryfile Summer Diaries
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor discovers how a secret wartime mission behind enemy lines brought one of our favourite seasonings, garlic, to the Isle of Wight. Keeley Donovan learns how a group of dedicated volunteers came to the dramatic rescue of a stranded sunburnt dolphin that was clinging to life(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell revisits a bungalow in Tipton, Dion Dublin meets father-and-daughter developers in Burnley and Martin Roberts examines the potential of three flats in Lewisham, south-east London(n)

programme.title: A Matter of Life and Debt
programme.desc: In St Ives, 24-year-old Tassy turns to the internet to crowd-fund a surf school, hoping to help local girls achieve their potential and increase their self-confidence(n)

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: Chris Bavin heads to Petworth in West Sussex to meet a group of residents entering Bloom for the first time who are hoping to get a disconnected community working together. These Bloomers want to engage the whole area, from the historic town centre to the modern housing estates on the outskirts, in an effort to bring the community spirit back to their neighbourhood(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp oversees proceedings as Tim Weeks and Richard Madley helps two teams look for items at the South of England Showground in Ardingly, West Sussex(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah is house hunting with a couple who have a budget of £650,000 and want to quit the city to find a house with great entertaining potential in the Cheshire countryside. Sonali also delves into the region's manufacturing heritage at one of the country's best preserved textile mills from the Industrial Revolution(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: After a successful time at Bredbury recycling centre, Sarah Moore hopes to generate some profit from two hidden gems. It's up to Josh and Oli to reimagine some discarded logs, while Sarah has to come up with a great idea to transform some old pipes into fabulous and desirable centerpieces(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein investigate the murder of a man in 1930s Lincolnshire. It was discovered that Arthur Major died as a result of strychnine poisoning and 42-year-old Ethel Major was arrested and charged with wilful murder. She protested her innocence and at trial was defended by a famous barrister, Norman Birkett KC, but the jury took just one hour to declare her guilty(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Anita Manning are at Culzean Castle in South Ayrshire, where they assess items including a selection of golf memorabilia and a trio of Moorcroft ceramics(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Scotland's Home of the Year
programme.desc: Judges Michael Angus, Anna Campbell-Jones and Kate Spiers come together to consider all seven finalists, with the seven home owners from across Scotland gathering at the House for an Art Lover venue in Glasgow. The judges whittle down the finalists to their top three properties, before deciding on an overall winner(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mel makes the final preparations to leave Walford and as she covers her tracks at work, her behaviour makes Sharon suspicious. After one too many drinks, Mick takes Linda home and Stuart reassures him regarding Callum's whereabouts. As Robbie hopelessly flirts with Ash, Iqra kisses her in front of everyone(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: The second part of a two-legged edition of the quiz, which sees former England striker Darren Bent, 1993 and 1999 world champion 110m hurdler Colin Jackson, world Taekwondo title-winner Bianca Walkden and Olympic heptathlon medallist Kelly Sotherton join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker(n)

programme.title: Hold the Sunset
programme.desc: With her annoying sister Joan safely packed off home, Edith is thrilled when Phil announces that Jack Dugdale has made an offer on his house and he has accepted. Now they can get on and plan their future together, starting by setting a date for their wedding. However, it transpires that romance is in the air for more than just the one couple. Roger, Georgie and Bob attend a children's party, but are soon sent packing, while the family's portrayal in Wendy's book continues to cause angst. Comedy, starring Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins and John Cleese(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests Denise Lewis, Richard Osman, Robert Rinder and Katherine Ryan to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves(n)

programme.title: The King's Speech (2010)
programme.desc: The younger son of George V struggles to cope with an uncontrollable stammer, prompting his wife to enlist the aid of an eccentric Australian speech therapist. The support and friendship of the doctor prove invaluable when a crisis places the repressed prince on the throne, just as the outbreak of the Second World War leaves the country in need of a strong king. Oscar-winning fact-based drama, starring Colin Firth, Geoffrey Rush, Helena Bonham Carter and Guy Pearce(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The discovery of a Roman sword with traces of blood on it is linked to an unidentified, headless corpse found near Heathrow in March 2008. It leads the team members to a lock-up belonging to a bodybuilder called Mark Rix, who died of a suspected heart attack the same year, so they start looking for connections between the original murder and Rix's death. They are helped in their investigation by anthropologist Fiona Kennedy, for whom Dan begins to develop feelings - just as he is told his wife might be discharged from the secure hospital where she has spent the past several years. Tracy-Ann Obermann and Liz White guest star(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Steph McGovern present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Anna Jones and Gary O'Hanlon, and special guest Emily Atack. Plus, archive clips featuring Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nigella Lawson(n)

programme.title: Mary Berry's Quick Cooking
programme.desc: The food writer demonstrates recipes that deliver on flavour but save on time, inspired by the cuisine and cooking culture of places she visits. Mary beginning by making her first-ever visit to Rome to explore a cuisine that she has always adored, trying a new take on traditional pizza, uncovering a hidden gem in the Jewish quarter and cooking a speedy Roman classic with Michelin-starred chef Cristina Bowerman. Mary also whips up bruschetta, speedy pasta dishes and an irresistible tiramisu(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Arsenal v Tottenham Hotspur in the north London derby at the Emirates Stadium(n)

programme.title: Live Rowing
programme.desc: Matthew Pinsent presents live coverage from Ottensheim in Austria, where the world's best rowers have the dual aim of winning medals and securing their places at next summer's Olympics in Tokyo. An impressive 23 nations won medals at the recent World Cup regatta, so competition will be stiff. Britain topped the medals table at the 2016 Olympics with three golds but didn't win in any of the Olympic classes in Bulgaria last year, with Italy finishing first in the medals table for the second year in a row. Today's finals include the men's and women's fours and quadruple sculls. Race commentary comes from Garry Herbert and James Cracknell(n)

programme.title: Athletics
programme.desc: The Weltklasse Zurich. Action from the 13th and penultimate meeting of the Diamond League series, held at the Stadion Letzigrund. Triple European champion and member of Team GB Dina Asher-Smith competed over 200m, likely to be her last run over the distance prior to the World Championships in Doha. The stellar line-up also included the likes of world record holders Renaud Lavillenie in the pole vault, Barbora Spotakova in the javelin and steeplechaser Beatrice Chepkoech(n)

programme.title: Sportscene Results
programme.desc: David Currie presents the classified results and a round-up of the afternoon's main football news. Plus, a look back at the week's talking points(n)

programme.title: Avengers Assemble (2012)
programme.desc: A mystical artefact is stolen from a top-secret military base by Thor's brother Loki, who plans to use it to bring about an alien invasion. Spymaster Nick Fury brings together a team of superheroes, including Iron Man, Thor, Captain America and the Hulk, to defend Earth from the impending attack. Action adventure uniting the Marvel comic-book characters, starring Robert Downey Jr, Chris Evans, Chris Hemsworth, Mark Ruffalo, Scarlett Johansson, Tom Hiddleston, Jeremy Renner and Samuel L Jackson(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman invite another selection of famous faces to take part in the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. As always, the challengers are tasked with coming up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. This time, all the contestants come from the world of comedy, as Ralph Ineson, John Thomson, Janine Duvitski, Tony Maudsley, Melvyn Hayes, Stuart McGugan, Vicki Pepperdine and Rosie Cavaliero take part(n)

programme.title: Who Dares Wins
programme.desc: Game show in which two pairs of complete strangers team up, each battling to outdo the other by listing as much as they know about a given subject. The first couple to win two rounds get to play for £50,000 and each time the champions defeat a new set of opponents their winnings mount up(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Duffy volunteers at a care home and finds out how overlooked some residents can feel, and she decides to express her concerns to Charlie. Meanwhile, David is devastated when his ex-wife Rosalene calls him because their son, Ollie has run away. David finally finds him on a bridge, but Ollie slips off and is rushed into the emergency department where he undergoes a dangerous procedure. Elsewhere, Ethan pulls some strings to try and get Effie on the CF trial, and Archie returns for his first day back at work(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Burnley v Liverpool at Turf Moor and Southampton v Manchester United at St Mary's Stadium. Jurgen Klopp's men won both of their encounters with Sean Dyche's Clarets last season, including a 3-1 victory on this ground in December. The Reds had to come from behind in that fixture, with goals from James Milner, Roberto Firmino and Xherdan Shaqiri cancelling out Jack Cork's opener. Plus, action from Chelsea v Sheffield United at Stamford Bridge, Crystal Palace v Aston Villa at Selhurst Park, Leicester City v Bournemouth at the King Power Stadium, Manchester City v Brighton & Hove Albion at the Etihad Stadium, Newcastle United v Watford at St James' Park and West Ham United v Norwich City at London Stadium(n)

programme.title: The Rap Game UK
programme.desc: In the second episode, the artists are split into two groups and challenged to collaborate on a track together, blending their egos, styles and concerts(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: A look ahead to the opening round of regular-season fixtures, including Chicago Bears v Green Bay Packers and New England Patriots v Pittsburgh Steelers(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Ben Thompson and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Burnley v Liverpool at Turf Moor and Southampton v Manchester United at St Mary's Stadium. Jurgen Klopp's men won both of their encounters with Sean Dyche's Clarets last season, including a 3-1 victory on this ground in December. programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break(n)

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Ria Hebden present topical debate covering religious, moral and ethical issues, with guest panellists and contributions from viewers(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned in the Kent seaside town of Margate, the Lancashire town of Carnforth, and a residence near Chesterfield(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones visits Burghley House near Stamford in Lincolnshire, discovering how Christianity is woven through its 450-year history and exploring some of its finest features(n)

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes(n)

programme.title: Hold the Sunset
programme.desc: With her annoying sister Joan safely packed off home, Edith is thrilled when Phil announces that Jack Dugdale has made an offer on his house and he has accepted. Now they can get on and plan their future together, starting by setting a date for their wedding. However, it transpires that romance is in the air for more than just the one couple. Roger, Georgie and Bob attend a children's party, but are soon sent packing, while the family's portrayal in Wendy's book continues to cause angst. Comedy, starring Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins and John Cleese(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a Co Durham couple hoping to move to North Yorkshire on a budget of £600,000, and also gets a masterclass in building dry stone walls(n)

programme.title: Shop Well for Less?
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Steph McGovern are in Milton Keynes to meet the Howarth family - one of the biggest spending families to ever feature on the programme - to help them change their shopping habits in an attempt to save money. A lack of time management means the family has slid into some bad shopping habits, often buying two or three of the same household items, and having recently renovated their home, the family find themselves with credit card debt. Can Alex and Steph get this family to rein in their spending and get on top of their debt?(n)

programme.title: Cinderella (2015)
programme.desc: An orphaned girl is treated as a servant by her cruel stepmother and her two daughters. Her life is changed for ever by the intervention of a kindly fairy godmother, who gives her the opportunity to attend a lavish royal ball and win the heart of a handsome prince. Romantic fantasy, starring Lily James, Cate Blanchett, Richard Madden and Helena Bonham Carter(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a Strictly Come Dancing version of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants Katya Jones, Joe McFadden, Debbie McGee, Giovanni Pernice, Ore Oduba, Oti Mabuse, Lesley Joseph and AJ Pritchard(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In a special edition marking 80 years since the first wave of evacuees were sent from towns and cities to the safety of the countryside, Steve Brown retraces his grandmother Rose's journey from Croydon to Cornwall. Sean Fletcher experiences life as an evacuee with schoolchildren at Acton Scott Historic Farm, while Margherita Taylor meets people who were relocated to Chatsworth House for safety during the war(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Nick Grimshaw and Emma Willis chat to celebrity guests and introduce stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich assist Emma and Steve, whose front garden in Hoveringham overlooking the River Trent, feels very much 'on display' to passers-by. The couple want greater want privacy without losing the precious river view and also wish to utilise a half-boat that they own in the design. Both Charlie and the siblings propose a similar clever idea to solve the privacy problem, a sunken courtyard garden. This only makes it harder for Emma and Steve to decide which design to pick(n)

The Reds had to come from behind in that fixture, with goals from James Milner, Roberto Firmino and Xherdan Shaqiri cancelling out Jack Cork's opener. Plus, action from Chelsea v Sheffield United at Stamford Bridge, Crystal Palace v Aston Villa at Selhurst Park, Leicester City v Bournemouth at the King Power Stadium, Manchester City v Brighton & Hove Albion at the Etihad Stadium, Newcastle United v Watford at St James' Park and West Ham United v Norwich City at London Stadium(n)

programme.title: The Rap Game UK
programme.desc: In the second episode, the artists are split into two groups and challenged to collaborate on a track together, blending their egos, styles and concerts(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: A look ahead to the opening round of regular-season fixtures, including Chicago Bears v Green Bay Packers and New England Patriots v Pittsburgh Steelers(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Ben Thompson and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Burnley v Liverpool at Turf Moor and Southampton v Manchester United at St Mary's Stadium. programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break(n)

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Ria Hebden present topical debate covering religious, moral and ethical issues, with guest panellists and contributions from viewers(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned in the Kent seaside town of Margate, the Lancashire town of Carnforth, and a residence near Chesterfield(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones visits Burghley House near Stamford in Lincolnshire, discovering how Christianity is woven through its 450-year history and exploring some of its finest features(n)

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes(n)

programme.title: Hold the Sunset
programme.desc: With her annoying sister Joan safely packed off home, Edith is thrilled when Phil announces that Jack Dugdale has made an offer on his house and he has accepted. Now they can get on and plan their future together, starting by setting a date for their wedding. However, it transpires that romance is in the air for more than just the one couple. Roger, Georgie and Bob attend a children's party, but are soon sent packing, while the family's portrayal in Wendy's book continues to cause angst. Comedy, starring Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins and John Cleese(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a Co Durham couple hoping to move to North Yorkshire on a budget of £600,000, and also gets a masterclass in building dry stone walls(n)

programme.title: Shop Well for Less?
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Steph McGovern are in Milton Keynes to meet the Howarth family - one of the biggest spending families to ever feature on the programme - to help them change their shopping habits in an attempt to save money. A lack of time management means the family has slid into some bad shopping habits, often buying two or three of the same household items, and having recently renovated their home, the family find themselves with credit card debt. Can Alex and Steph get this family to rein in their spending and get on top of their debt?(n)

programme.title: Cinderella (2015)
programme.desc: An orphaned girl is treated as a servant by her cruel stepmother and her two daughters. Her life is changed for ever by the intervention of a kindly fairy godmother, who gives her the opportunity to attend a lavish royal ball and win the heart of a handsome prince. Romantic fantasy, starring Lily James, Cate Blanchett, Richard Madden and Helena Bonham Carter(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a Strictly Come Dancing version of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants Katya Jones, Joe McFadden, Debbie McGee, Giovanni Pernice, Ore Oduba, Oti Mabuse, Lesley Joseph and AJ Pritchard(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In a special edition marking 80 years since the first wave of evacuees were sent from towns and cities to the safety of the countryside, Steve Brown retraces his grandmother Rose's journey from Croydon to Cornwall. Sean Fletcher experiences life as an evacuee with schoolchildren at Acton Scott Historic Farm, while Margherita Taylor meets people who were relocated to Chatsworth House for safety during the war(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are at Morden Hall Park in South London, assessing items including a rare Cartier driver's watch, a self-portrait by John Lennon and Yoko Ono and a plate designed by Pablo Picasso. Jewellery specialist Joanna Hardy examines a diamond studded replica of a brooch originally designed for Wallis Simpson, while glass expert Andy McConnell inspects three early 18th-century pieces bought for just a few pence(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Tommy gathers everyone together to discuss recent tragic events and to give his orders, before the Shelby men travel to London to meet Mosley and it's soon revealed who has the upper hand. Linda heads to Arrow House to ask Lizzie's advice, Polly visits Aberama to pass on instructions from Tommy, who learns that one of the Peaky Blinders is in danger and sends Arthur and Johnny Dogs on an urgent mission to intervene. Period crime drama, starring Cillian Murphy, Helen McCrory and Sam Claflin(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, featuring Arsenal v Tottenham Hotspur at Emirates Stadium and Everton v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Goodison Park. The Gunners had not lost at home to their north London rivals since 2010 and won this fixture 4-2 last season, while Wolves were looking to emulate their 3-1 victory away to the Toffees last term. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's goals(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: The second part of a two-legged edition of the quiz, which sees former England striker Darren Bent, 1993 and 1999 world champion 110m hurdler Colin Jackson, world Taekwondo title-winner Bianca Walkden and Olympic heptathlon medallist Kelly Sotherton join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker(n)

programme.title: Dealbhan Fraoich (Heather's Portraits)
programme.desc: Islay-born artist Heather Dewar draws a charcoal portrait of journalist, author and educator Roddy Maclean. Although Roddy now lives in Inverness, he was born in Africa and spent much of his life in Tasmania before settling in Scotland, his father's homeland(n)

programme.title: Cuimhneachan (Remembrance)
programme.desc: The legacy of the Great War through the eyes of the bards who were writing in the years after the conflict. It was thought it would be 'the war to end all wars', but it wasn't and that realisation led many poets such as Murdo MacFarlane to question the effectiveness of war in general. However, at the same time it was realised that the sacrifice of those who went into battle should never be forgotten. Featuring specially recorded performances by Gaelic singers Art Cormack, Linda MacLeod, Murdo MacDonald and Robert Robertson(n)

programme.title: An Dotair Mòr
programme.desc: Documentary about a doctor who in the 1930s managed to treat patients living in remote locations, despite there being no hospitals, telephones and few roads. He used any means possible, and often travelled either by boat, on foot or on horseback in an attempt to save lives(n)

programme.title: Belladrum

programme.desc: Drama following three lives that cross paths, intertwine and let go of each other as destined, each seeking forgiveness, freedom and a new beginning(n)

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Paul Potts, the first ever winner of Britain's Got Talent in 2007, is set to return for the all-star series. Here, he talks about the competition and his journey, from the brink of bankruptcy to the big screen and now the life of a multi-millionaire(n)

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip(n)

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom(n)

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more(n)

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Guest panellists engage in topical debate and celebrity interviews from a female perspective(n)

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David and his team head to Clitheroe in Lancashire, where dealer Debbie Serpell encounters Andy Pandy. Simon Schneider tries to snap up an art nouveau photo frame and James Layte hopes he can produce enough cash to secure a pretty Chatelaine(n)

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis. A team of five from Holyhead take on the Tenable Tower as they attempt to win a £125,000 prize. Can they score a Perfect 10 to bring home the money?(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shepherd, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Gregg Wallace, Denise Lewis, Mark Pougatch and Gina Yashere pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jessie struggles to stay positive, Moira is hit by a pang of jealousy, and Jacob offers his support(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jan reveals his intention to lie low in Birmingham until after the court case and urges Eileen to come with him, and Evelyn resorts to extreme measures when she hears there's no room for Hope at the local school. When Geoff turns up for work at Speed Daal, Yasmeen refuses to let him lift a finger(n)

programme.title: Rescue - River Deep, Mountain High
programme.desc: In the Cairngorm mountains in Scotland, a group of handlers and their search and rescue dogs undergo final training in the snowy peaks. Every search and rescue dog will train for up to two years before it is deemed ready to act within mountain rescue teams looking for lost and injured walkers. In the Galtee Mountains of Tipperary, Search and Rescue dog Ziggy is searching for an injured walker somewhere near Galtee mountain, while in the Peak District the Kinder Mountain Rescue Team attends a walker in severe pain with a broken ankle(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: The police and a reporter turn up at the school as Tyrone pleads with Evelyn to see sense, and Robert says he has CCTV of Jed trying to blackmail him. Realising Tim is in the dark, Yasmeen urges Geoff to tell him about his illness(n)

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Cole's former mentor from his CIA years Tom Barnes appears with some alarming news, as he and Murtaugh set about investigating the robbery of safe deposit boxes, whose suspects seem connected to his past. Meanwhile, Murtaugh encounters his wife's new client Louie Gutierrez. 'The Gute', as she is colloquially known, immediately proves to be a strong adversary, causing upset with every move she makes, prompting him to enlist Bailey's help to investigate her. Action drama, starring Damon Wayans and Seann William Scott, with Chandler Kinney(n)

programme.title: The Hobbit: The Desolation of Smaug (2013)
programme.desc: The party of dwarves and their hobbit ally continue the quest to reclaim their lost kingdom, journeying through the forest home of ancestral enemy the elves and finally facing the dragon that drove them from their home. Meanwhile, Gandalf the wizard investigates the rise of a mysterious dark power. Part two of Peter Jackson's fantasy adventure trilogy based on JRR Tolkien's novel, starring Martin Freeman, Ian McKellen, Richard Armitage and Orlando Bloom(n)

programme.title: Saved
programme.desc: The stories of two car accidents which took place at night, beginning with the experience of 18-year-old care worker Dannie Phillips, whose vehicle veered off the road in Cornwall on Christmas Eve 2012 and started sinking in a murky quagmire. The second case features a family of four on their way to a holiday in Devon, who suffered a nightmare scenario when a tyre blowout left their car in the central reservation of the M5 and at the mercy of oncoming traffic on the carriageway(n)

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Super 4
programme.desc: Animated comedy adventure series featuring a gang of heroes who protect the city of Technopolis and its King Kenric against evil elements(n)

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: Part one of two. Gordon ventures deep into the Mariana trench to rescue a survey team, and ends up discovering an early International Rescue prototype once flown by Jeff Tracy(n)

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Bean plans a romantic meal with his girlfriend, only to fall asleep while the main course is in the oven. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson(n)

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Bean is forced to retrieve a tennis ball from the roof of a house across the road, but gets paralysed by vertigo 60ft up and has to rely on a hamster to steer him to safety. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson(n)

programme.title: Marvel's Avengers Assemble
programme.desc: After Loki becomes the Sorcerer Supreme, the Avengers are the only thing standing between him and domination of the universe(n)

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: When Ben and Max get into an argument, Ben goes Heatblast to blow off steam against Max's wishes; but Dr. Animo spots him and subsequently turns Max into a chicken(n)

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: After Ben stumbles upon a video of Cannonbolt ripping up a computer lab on the internet, Team Tennyson must race to State U where Ben discovers a steam-punk version of his alien(n)

programme.title: The Sara Cox Show
programme.desc: Sara is joined by guests Kris Marshall, Jack Fox, Sara Pascoe and Emma Bullimore. Plus, live music from new band Superbird and more suggestions of things to do this weekend(n)

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined by presenter Jenni Falconer and chef John Williams. On the menu are chicken and tarragon pie, ratatouille, and a lemon and courgette risotto(n)

programme.title: Save Money: Good Health
programme.desc: Sian Williams puts home bacteria-testing kits to the test, and examines various products to determine if changes to diet can bring about similar effects. Ranj Singh takes a look at digestive ailments such as acid reflux and indigestion and recommends the best products for treating them. Plus, a guide to preventing food poisoning and treating ingrown toenails(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and his team of experts, Katie Rushworth, Frances Tophill and David Domoney, creates a modern family garden in Hucknall near Nottingham. The project is a surprise for a landscape gardener who was diagnosed with Alzheimer's at the age of just 32, making him one of the youngest people in the country to develop the condition and leaving him physically unable to complete the garden personally. Alan also provides viewers with some tips on sensory planting and creating home-made play equipment for children(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Coma victim Harriet Farmer finally wakes up a year after she was attacked while trying to elope, but her boyfriend Finn Robson is still missing. Soon after she regains consciousness, members of a chess club are targeted by a killer. Could the assault that left Harriet for dead be linked to moves on a games board? Guest starring Fay Ripley (Cold Feet), with Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which a team of five family members from Northumberland answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge. They then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: Zone of Champions
programme.desc: Comedy clip show, in which sports commentators played by John Thomson and Jessica Knappett narrate home movie footage as if they were sporting events. Featuring coverage of Dog Jenga, the Alpine Calamity and Wilfully Dangerous Snooker.(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity edition of the game show, asking contestants Anton Du Beke, Gemma Collins and Paul Sinha to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. After the first elimination, the two remaining players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000 for their chosen charity(n)

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent: The Champions
programme.desc: Ant and Dec present a special version of the talent show, in which stars from the past 13 series of Britain's Got Talent compete against winners from versions of the programme from around the world to impress the viewers and judges Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon and David Walliams. The first episode features the return of Paul Potts and Boogie Storm, as well as Ukraine's Kseniya Simonova and father-daughter duo Bello and Annaliese Nock from the US(n)

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the big-prize quiz, in which another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with lifelines, including Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend and 50-50 available to them if they get stuck(n)

programme.title: Rambo III (1988)
programme.desc: Vietnam veteran John Rambo turns down a request from his old colonel to join a mission in Soviet-occupied Afghanistan. However, when the officer is captured, Rambo sets off to save him, joining the Mujahedeen rebels in fighting against the Russian invaders. Action adventure sequel, starring Sylvester Stallone, Richard Crenna, Kurtwood Smith, Marc de Jonge and Sasson Gabai(n)

programme.title: Britain's Best Back Gardens
programme.desc: In celebration of his 50 years as a horticulturist, Alan Titchmarsh has spent the past 12 months appealing to the public to nominate their gardens, from which he has chosen his top 30. In this final programme, he presents his 10 favourite flights of fancy - the most creative outdoor spaces he has seen during the nationwide search, from the brash and the bonkers to the brilliantly over the top(n)

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Super 4
programme.desc: Animated comedy adventure series featuring a gang of heroes who protect the city of Technopolis and its King Kenric against evil elements(n)

programme.title: Super 4
programme.desc: Alex falls asleep while guarding his father's castle, allowing Sykronior to turn Kingsland into his new holiday home(n)

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: Part two of two. International Rescue launches a full-scale operation to recover a stolen prototype from The Mechanic(n)

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Eating too many sweets gives Bean a terrible toothache, and when all other attempts at curing it are exhausted, he reluctantly visits the dreaded dentist(n)

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: The halfwit becomes a hero after rescuing a kitten from a tree, and looks forward to having his picture in the local paper. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson(n)

programme.title: LEGO Jurassic World: Secret Exhibit
programme.desc: For Owen Grady it was an easy job: deliver the dinosaurs and get paid. Nothing could possibly go wrong, could it? Find out what happened before the movie in Jurassic World: The Secret Exhibit!(n)

programme.title: The Sara Cox Show
programme.desc: Sara is joined by Loose Women's Nadia Sawalha and Saira Khan, and author Adele Parks. Plus, live music by Scouting for Girls and recommendations for the weekend(n)

programme.title: Ninja Warrior UK
programme.desc: Competitors from across the nation test their strength, speed and courage in this obstacle-course challenge, hosted by Ben Shephard, Rochelle Humes and Chris Kamara. Members of the public compete alongside celebrities Rickie Williams, Melvin Odoom and Laura Crane, and those who make it to the end of the adrenaline-fuelled mission face one final task - scaling the heights of a tower named Mount Midoriyama - as they battle it out for the title of Ninja Warrior UK(n)

programme.title: Countrywise
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin takes a canoe journey along the River Wye, meandering along the border of England and Wales where the river offers up a wealth of wildlife including one of Britain's most elusive and mysterious birds - the night jar. Ben Fogle heads to the Exmoor coast and the picturesque village of Porlock, where he samples the locally farmed oysters that are making a comeback. Plus, the programme revisits Britain's biggest farming family, the Owens in the Yorkshire Dales, to see how baby number nine Nancy is settling in(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Nelson discover that the exciting world of stunt pilots and military heroes hides many dark secrets after the owner of Finchmere Airfield is hit on the head and dropped to his death from a plane. Are anti-aircraft campaigners behind Bernard King's murder, or is the incident linked to a tragic Second World War mission? Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee are joined by guest stars including June Whitfield, Robert Bathurst, Bernard Cribbins and Laila Rouass(n)

programme.desc: Comedy clip show, in which sports commentators played by John Thomson and Jessica Knappett narrate home movie footage as if they were sporting events. Featuring coverage of Dog Jenga, the Alpine Calamity and Wilfully Dangerous Snooker.(n)

Contestants Jimmy Carr and Rachel Riley compete against each other to answer visual questions by placing rings on an LED floor in five increasingly difficult stages, hoping to win £50,000 for their teams(n)

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the big-prize quiz, in which another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with lifelines, including Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend and 50-50 available to them if they get stuck(n)

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: Charlotte and Sidney clash again, only for Charlotte to be left confused by a chance encounter. Tensions surrounding Miss Lambe's arrival in Sanditon erupt at Lady Denham's grand pineapple luncheon, as the hostess makes disparaging comments about Miss Lambe's race, and Esther and Edward realise that Clara poses a significant threat to their ambitions. Drama adapted from the unfinished novel by Jane Austen, starring Rose Williams, Theo James, Anne Reid, Charlotte Spencer, Kris Marshall and Kate Ashfield(n)

programme.title: The Best Little Prison in Britain?
programme.desc: Head of Security Margo receives intel that there may be two mobile phones on the wing, and attempts to hunt them down unsmiling her new phone detecting pole. Meanwhile, George Store picks up sweet supplies from the local supermarket, and an inmate is granted a home visit as a reward for his good behaviour(n)

programme.title: Deep Water
programme.desc: Guy is arrested for his attack on Nelson, and Joanne digs deeper into his past. Traumatised by events and still desperate to find Lucinda, he returns home to Kate. Meanwhile, Lisa opens up to Roz about Adam, but Roz is unable to reveal her own secret in return. Lisa and Joe come together to tackle a crisis at the kennels, and assuming that she has Joe's support, Lisa begins to hope for a new future away from her problems with the Rivertys. Starring Anna Friel, Sinead Keenan and Rosalind Eleazar(n)

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the big-prize quiz, in which another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with lifelines, including Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend and 50-50 available to them if they get stuck(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom(n)

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Paul Potts, the first ever winner of Britain's Got Talent in 2007, is set to return for the all-star series. Here, he talks about the competition and his journey, from the brink of bankruptcy to the big screen and now the life of a multi-millionaire(n)

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip(n)

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom(n)

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more(n)

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Guest panellists engage in topical debate and celebrity interviews from a female perspective(n)

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David and his team head to Clitheroe in Lancashire, where dealer Debbie Serpell encounters Andy Pandy. Simon Schneider tries to snap up an art nouveau photo frame and James Layte hopes he can produce enough cash to secure a pretty Chatelaine(n)

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis. A team of five from Holyhead take on the Tenable Tower as they attempt to win a £125,000 prize. Can they score a Perfect 10 to bring home the money?(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shepherd, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special

They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jessie struggles to stay positive, Moira is hit by a pang of jealousy, and Jacob offers his support(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jan reveals his intention to lie low in Birmingham until after the court case and urges Eileen to come with him, and Evelyn resorts to extreme measures when she hears there's no room for Hope at the local school. When Geoff turns up for work at Speed Daal, Yasmeen refuses to let him lift a finger(n)

programme.title: Rescue - River Deep, Mountain High
programme.desc: In the Cairngorm mountains in Scotland, a group of handlers and their search and rescue dogs undergo final training in the snowy peaks. Every search and rescue dog will train for up to two years before it is deemed ready to act within mountain rescue teams looking for lost and injured walkers. In the Galtee Mountains of Tipperary, Search and Rescue dog Ziggy is searching for an injured walker somewhere near Galtee mountain, while in the Peak District the Kinder Mountain Rescue Team attends a walker in severe pain with a broken ankle(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: The police and a reporter turn up at the school as Tyrone pleads with Evelyn to see sense, and Robert says he has CCTV of Jed trying to blackmail him. Realising Tim is in the dark, Yasmeen urges Geoff to tell him about his illness(n)

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Cole's former mentor from his CIA years Tom Barnes appears with some alarming news, as he and Murtaugh set about investigating the robbery of safe deposit boxes, whose suspects seem connected to his past. Meanwhile, Murtaugh encounters his wife's new client Louie Gutierrez. 'The Gute', as she is colloquially known, immediately proves to be a strong adversary, causing upset with every move she makes, prompting him to enlist Bailey's help to investigate her. Action drama, starring Damon Wayans and Seann William Scott, with Chandler Kinney(n)

programme.title: The Hobbit: The Desolation of Smaug (2013)
programme.desc: The party of dwarves and their hobbit ally continue the quest to reclaim their lost kingdom, journeying through the forest home of ancestral enemy the elves and finally facing the dragon that drove them from their home. Meanwhile, Gandalf the wizard investigates the rise of a mysterious dark power. Part two of Peter Jackson's fantasy adventure trilogy based on JRR Tolkien's novel, starring Martin Freeman, Ian McKellen, Richard Armitage and Orlando Bloom(n)

programme.title: Saved
programme.desc: The stories of two car accidents which took place at night, beginning with the experience of 18-year-old care worker Dannie Phillips, whose vehicle veered off the road in Cornwall on Christmas Eve 2012 and started sinking in a murky quagmire. The second case features a family of four on their way to a holiday in Devon, who suffered a nightmare scenario when a tyre blowout left their car in the central reservation of the M5 and at the mercy of oncoming traffic on the carriageway(n)

programme.desc: Part one of two. Gordon ventures deep into the Mariana trench to rescue a survey team, and ends up discovering an early International Rescue prototype once flown by Jeff Tracy(n)

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Bean plans a romantic meal with his girlfriend, only to fall asleep while the main course is in the oven. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson(n)

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Bean is forced to retrieve a tennis ball from the roof of a house across the road, but gets paralysed by vertigo 60ft up and has to rely on a hamster to steer him to safety. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson(n)

programme.title: Marvel's Avengers Assemble
programme.desc: After Loki becomes the Sorcerer Supreme, the Avengers are the only thing standing between him and domination of the universe(n)

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: When Ben and Max get into an argument, Ben goes Heatblast to blow off steam against Max's wishes; but Dr. Animo spots him and subsequently turns Max into a chicken(n)

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: After Ben stumbles upon a video of Cannonbolt ripping up a computer lab on the internet, Team Tennyson must race to State U where Ben discovers a steam-punk version of his alien(n)

programme.title: The Sara Cox Show
programme.desc: Sara is joined by guests Kris Marshall, Jack Fox, Sara Pascoe and Emma Bullimore. Plus, live music from new band Superbird and more suggestions of things to do this weekend(n)

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined by presenter Jenni Falconer and chef John Williams. On the menu are chicken and tarragon pie, ratatouille, and a lemon and courgette risotto(n)

programme.title: Save Money: Good Health
programme.desc: Sian Williams puts home bacteria-testing kits to the test, and examines various products to determine if changes to diet can bring about similar effects. Ranj Singh takes a look at digestive ailments such as acid reflux and indigestion and recommends the best products for treating them. Plus, a guide to preventing food poisoning and treating ingrown toenails(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and his team of experts, Katie Rushworth, Frances Tophill and David Domoney, creates a modern family garden in Hucknall near Nottingham. The project is a surprise for a landscape gardener who was diagnosed with Alzheimer's at the age of just 32, making him one of the youngest people in the country to develop the condition and leaving him physically unable to complete the garden personally. Alan also provides viewers with some tips on sensory planting and creating home-made play equipment for children(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The master of Gresham College, amateur astronomer Andrew Crompton, is found dead at the Oxford University observatory, and Lewis finds there are numerous suspects, from senior academics to college scouts. Laura Hobson's involvement with a local orchestra helps her give the detective inside information on the complex relationships within the close-knit community, and Hathaway's sharp mind cracks an astronomical conundrum as the team slowly unravels the riddle of the victim's death. Guest starring Sophie Ward (Heartbeat), Diana Quick (Brideshead Revisited) and Robert Hardy (Cornelius Fudge in the Harry Potter films)(n)

Molesley is concerned to see Daisy becoming disillusioned with her studies, and Isobel makes a happy announcement, but there may be trouble on the horizon. programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. After seven years of ducking the issue, Lewis and Hobson are embarking on a relationship, but the detective also has a new case to solve. Elderly don Richard Seager is murdered on the night of his release from prison, having served a sentence for causing death by dangerous driving. Crushed by a classic car, Seager mysteriously scratched the number 500 into its paintwork before his demise, and the academic's wife suspects a member of his victim's family is responsible for the killing. With Stephen Churchett, Alison Steadman and Edward Fox(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War

Police drama, starring Michael Kitchen, with Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An American businessman fears his life is in danger while travelling on the Orient Express from Istanbul to Paris, and offers fellow passenger Poirot $10,000 for protection. After rejecting the proposal, the sleuth wakes up the following morning to learn the man has been stabbed to death while the train was held up by heavy snow in Serbia. Racing against time to find the killer among the passengers, Poirot must look to the past to solve the crime, and is faced with a decision that will haunt him for ever. David Suchet takes the title role in the murder mystery, with guest stars Hugh Bonneville, David Morrissey and Eileen Atkins(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is invited to attend a masquerade ball dressed as someone famous and modestly goes as himself - and finds time amid the revelry to solve the murder of aristocratic fellow guest Lord Cronshaw. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A student production of The Merchant of Venice is halted when the actor playing Shylock is murdered during the performance. The detectives discover the victim had an expensive lifestyle and not only owed money to his fellow students, but was also suspected of stealing from them. However, the investigation has barely got under way before the killer strikes again. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star(n)

Mystery, starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Jan Harvey, Martin Jarvis and James Hazeldine(n)

However, what Dr Ellingham's friends and family fail to realise is that he is actually trapped up at the Winton farm, where desperate wife Annie is trying to coerce him into performing a life-saving operation on her terminally ill husband Jim. programme.desc: When Hastings and a reluctant Poirot attend a grouse shoot at a friend's estate, one of the hunting party is murdered. Despite suffering a severe cold, the Belgian sleuth is roused from his sickbed to solve the case. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.desc: A profile of the singer filmed over a six-month period at his home in Portugal, at Abbey Road Studios and at Wimbledon, examining his career highs and lows and his record-breaking longevity. Cliff talks about the dark days that followed the police raid on his home in 2014, his battle to clear his name and the people whose support have got him through tough times. Featuring interviews with Barry Gibb, Olivia Newton-John, Hank Marvin, Jimmy Osmond, Andrew Lloyd Webber, Brian May and Joan Collins(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly worries about Angie, Bet's jealousy shows on Liz's last day at the Rovers, and Raquel thanks Gordon for treating her like a lady(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Ventress has his work cut out as a series of crimes linked to an old farming feud turns deadly. But when the evidence fails to add up it is up to the bobbies to prove who is responsible before someone gets killed. Meanwhile, Vernon is inspired to screen films in the village hall. Rural police drama, starring Geoffrey Hughes, William Simons, Tricia Penrose and Jason Durr(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Part one of two. Jessica visits an old Irish castle, but her trip coincides with the discovery of a missing girl's body and a plan by an American to turn the building into a first-class hotel. The sleuth also hears a tale about the possible illegal sale of ancient treasures. Guest starring Rod Taylor (The Birds), with Angela Lansbury(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Two women vie for the inspector's affections convinced the stars foretell love. Classic Seventies comedy, starring Stephen Lewis and Bob Grant(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Inspector Blake gives Jack a new clippie, knowing her incessant chatter will drive him barmy. Seventies comedy, starring Bob Grant and Stephen Lewis(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Ruth talks her cantankerous landlord Rigsby into renting his newly vacated room to a friend of hers. Pilot episode of the classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour and Don Warrington(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Religious fanatic Steven Mullan is found beaten and drowned in his bath. Lewis and Hathaway discover he had been imprisoned for trying to kill well-known atheist Tom Rattan and had only recently been released. The inspector soon has reason to believe the Rattan family is hiding something from him - but nothing prepares him for the evidence Dr Hobson uncovers at the post-mortem. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star, with guest appearances by Jenny Seagrove and Julian Wadham(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A mysterious visitor is suspected of involvement in the theft of a priceless Ming vase. Mike tries to discover the reason for Merton's visit to headquarters and Gina starts acting like Aidensfield's answer to Mata Hari. Nostalgic drama, guest starring Leslie Grantham(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim is touchy when Joe mentions Neil to her, Archie and Seth's truffle-hunting venture begins in earnest, and Lorraine has some shocking news for Lynn(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Lorraine is determined to inform the authorities about her father, Jack is not happy with Mark's work ethic, and Eric tells Elizabeth she will have to face Michael soon(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim and Liz move into the Queen's, Angie breaks down when Neil leaves, and Gordon does not want to rush things with Raquel(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Denise puts Fiona off when she asks for a rise, Angie is depressed about her future, and Don takes Martin to the Queen's to celebrate his birthday(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The master of Gresham College, amateur astronomer Andrew Crompton, is found dead at the Oxford University observatory, and Lewis finds there are numerous suspects, from senior academics to college scouts. Laura Hobson's involvement with a local orchestra helps her give the detective inside information on the complex relationships within the close-knit community, and Hathaway's sharp mind cracks an astronomical conundrum as the team slowly unravels the riddle of the victim's death. Guest starring Sophie Ward (Heartbeat), Diana Quick (Brideshead Revisited) and Robert Hardy (Cornelius Fudge in the Harry Potter films)(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Secrets could be exposed as Rosamund arrives at Downton following Edith's shock departure. Rose is delighted to introduce Atticus and his parents to the Abbey, while Anna and Bates start thinking about a fresh start, now that the worst of their problems are seemingly behind them. Molesley is concerned to see Daisy becoming disillusioned with her studies, and Isobel makes a happy announcement, but there may be trouble on the horizon. Violet has her hands full with her warring servants, while Mary and Blake seize an opportunity to send Gillingham a strong message(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Part two of two. Jessica manages to break out of the castle dungeon - but the murderer is still at large. Whodunnit, guest starring Rod Taylor (The Birds) with Angela Lansbury,(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. After seven years of ducking the issue, Lewis and Hobson are embarking on a relationship, but the detective also has a new case to solve. Elderly don Richard Seager is murdered on the night of his release from prison, having served a sentence for causing death by dangerous driving. Crushed by a classic car, Seager mysteriously scratched the number 500 into its paintwork before his demise, and the academic's wife suspects a member of his victim's family is responsible for the killing. With Stephen Churchett, Alison Steadman and Edward Fox(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The year is 1940, and Sam is made homeless when a bomb destroys her boarding house, alerting Foyle to a system of organised looting around Hastings. However, when a corpse is found on the beach, the investigation brings the team into conflict with British attempts to secure vital American aid for the war effort. Police drama, starring Michael Kitchen, with Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An American businessman fears his life is in danger while travelling on the Orient Express from Istanbul to Paris, and offers fellow passenger Poirot $10,000 for protection. After rejecting the proposal, the sleuth wakes up the following morning to learn the man has been stabbed to death while the train was held up by heavy snow in Serbia. Racing against time to find the killer among the passengers, Poirot must look to the past to solve the crime, and is faced with a decision that will haunt him for ever. David Suchet takes the title role in the murder mystery, with guest stars Hugh Bonneville, David Morrissey and Eileen Atkins(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is invited to attend a masquerade ball dressed as someone famous and modestly goes as himself - and finds time amid the revelry to solve the murder of aristocratic fellow guest Lord Cronshaw. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: In the aftermath of the storm, Rowan takes charge of the situation as the residents try to free Charlotte from beneath the rubble and rescue Danny, who has been left behind by Alice in her desperate search for help. Vanessa and Rowan use a helicopter to eventually spot him lying unconscious in the Bush and take him home. The next morning, Alice decides to pack her bags and leave, but a kiss from Danny might prompt a change of heart(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: Danny and Alice try to keep their new romance a secret, but have more pressing concerns when she is attacked by a lioness - one of several animals on the loose after the storm. Relations between Leopard's Den and Mara reach breaking point when Vanessa refuses to supply drugs to help dying elephant calf Tula, while Du Plessis is delighted when Caroline arrives at the party(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan is forced to change his unhealthy diet after the drivers are obliged to take a medical test. Vintage Seventies comedy, starring Reg Varney and Stephen Lewis(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: A valuable heirloom brought back from the China Sea could change the Ropers' fortunes - but not if Mildred's sister gets it first. Starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce(n)

programme.title: Carry On Again Doctor (1969)
programme.desc: A disgraced surgeon sets up a clinic in the hope of promoting a supposedly miraculous slimming potion made from secret ingredients discovered on a remote tropical island. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Jim Dale, Barbara Windsor, Charles Hawtrey, Hattie Jacques, Pat Coombs and Joan Sims(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Mischievous artist Harry Field, who specialises in preparing bogus coats of arms for gullible American tourists, is murdered - and Morse takes a personal interest in finding the culprit. But as investigations progress, he discovers the victim was not quite the lovable rogue he initially appeared. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately, Geraldine James and Freddie Jones(n)

programme.title: The Wizard of Oz (1939)
programme.desc: Youngster Dorothy Gale is whisked over the rainbow by a tornado into the magical land of Oz, where she inadvertently kills a wicked witch - incurring the wrath of another. Setting out to find the wizard who can show her a way home, she is joined on her journey by a scarecrow, a tin man and a cowardly lion, who all hope their own wishes will be granted. Musical fantasy, with Judy Garland, Ray Bolger, Jack Haley, Bert Lahr and Frank Morgan(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The master of Gresham College, amateur astronomer Andrew Crompton, is found dead at the Oxford University observatory, and Lewis finds there are numerous suspects, from senior academics to college scouts. Laura Hobson's involvement with a local orchestra helps her give the detective inside information on the complex relationships within the close-knit community, and Hathaway's sharp mind cracks an astronomical conundrum as the team slowly unravels the riddle of the victim's death. Guest starring Sophie Ward (Heartbeat), Diana Quick (Brideshead Revisited) and Robert Hardy (Cornelius Fudge in the Harry Potter films)(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A barmaid's murder leads Lewis (Kevin Whately) and Hathaway (Laurence Fox) to a respected professor, who admits he once had an affair with the woman. However, they discover the killing was copied straight from the pages of a popular fantasy novel written by an Oxford tutor, suggesting it may be the work of a crazed fan - or something even more sinister. Guest starring Art Malik, Tom Mison and James Fox, father of Laurence(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The year is 1940, and Sam is made homeless when a bomb destroys her boarding house, alerting Foyle to a system of organised looting around Hastings. However, when a corpse is found on the beach, the investigation brings the team into conflict with British attempts to secure vital American aid for the war effort. Police drama, starring Michael Kitchen, with Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Captain Hastings is staying with friends when wealthy heiress Mrs Inglethorp is murdered. He calls in Hercule Poirot to help him solve the baffling crime, which has the whole household under suspicion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet as the Belgian sleuth, with Hugh Fraser, Pauline Moran, Anthony Calf and Philip Jackson(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The leader of a censorship campaign comes to Oxford on a speaking tour, and Morse is charged with protecting her in light of a series of death threats. A bricklayer working on a controversial pop singer's house is later found dead, and when a second killing occurs, a connection emerges to the speaker's movement, plunging Morse into the middle of a conflict over the nature of British culture(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The discovery of a woman's body in a car park presents Jack Frost with another challenge to his deductive powers. Delving into the victim's electronic organiser, he learns she was connected with a local religious community - and realises someone in the congregation is more interested in the pleasures of this world than the rewards of the next. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander and John Lyons(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Carol is pleased that social services are investigating Nick and Archie, and Joe is hit over the head when he hears some burglars(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Joe wakes up in hospital with Jack beside him, Frank is grateful to Neil for looking after Kim, and Jack thinks Sarah might propose to him on their anniversary(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: The depot football team are promised substantial cash bonuses if they can win their match against the Basildon Bashers. However, Stan manages to injure his own side's star player and a surprise lies in store when they meet their opponents. Reg Varney stars(n)

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: When Matron is attacked by a thief, everyone is surprised to discover who was responsible - but there appears to be more to the case than meets the eye. Goodwin is upset when it becomes clear he has acquired a bad reputation around the hospital and the staff worry about the threat of a tuberculosis epidemic. Medical drama, starring Wendy Craig, Paul Fox, Robert Daws and Amy Robbins(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Steve is forced to cover for Phil when the father-to-be discovers Gina has gone into labour two months early and rushes to the hospital to help with the nerve-racking delivery. Meanwhile, Merton is excited when it appears a drug dealer could soon be brought to justice. Drama, starring Mark Jordon, Tricia Penrose and Duncan Bell(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Jenny falls victim to conmen who have devised an elaborate loan and savings racket to fleece unwitting villagers. Meanwhile, Vernon, Bernie and David put their literary prowess to the test by entering a poetry competition run by the Ashfordly Gazette. Rural drama, starring Sarah Tansey and Geoffrey Hughes(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A student production of The Merchant of Venice is halted when the actor playing Shylock is murdered during the performance. Lewis and Hathaway are struck by the lack of grief among the cast and crew - especially the ambitious director, who wastes no time recasting the role. The detectives discover the victim had an expensive lifestyle and not only owed money to his fellow students, but was also suspected of stealing from them. However, the investigation has barely got under way before the killer strikes again. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Religious fanatic Steven Mullan is found beaten and drowned in his bath. programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford sleuth investigates a double mystery connected to the local Greek community - the death of a restaurant chef and the disappearance of a baby - and wonders whether an academic event attended by some of the university's leading classical scholars could shed light on the case. Mystery, starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Jan Harvey, Martin Jarvis and James Hazeldine(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth has retired to a charming English village in the hope of finding peace and quiet. However, the murder of a rich industrialist neighbour forces him to leave his new lifestyle behind as he plunges into a sordid world of blackmail and envy, aided by the dogged Chief Inspector Japp. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, Philip Jackson, Malcolm Terris and Oliver Ford Davies(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa worries Dr Timoney's suggestion that she and Martin aren't meant to be together might have prompted him to leave Portwenn for good. Calling on Ruth, Morwenna and Janice for help, she tries to track the down the missing GP, with no luck. However, what Dr Ellingham's friends and family fail to realise is that he is actually trapped up at the Winton farm, where desperate wife Annie is trying to coerce him into performing a life-saving operation on her terminally ill husband Jim. Meanwhile, Ruth and Bert seek out Al to offer him a proposition regarding the whisky and the B&B. Medical drama, starring Martin Clunes(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When Hastings and a reluctant Poirot attend a grouse shoot at a friend's estate, one of the hunting party is murdered. Despite suffering a severe cold, the Belgian sleuth is roused from his sickbed to solve the case. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An archaeologist dies of a heart attack shortly after smashing the seal of an ancient Egyptian tomb, raising the spectre of the mummy's curse. The grieving widow summons the Belgian sleuth to unravel the mystery, but he can only smoke out the real villain by risking his own life. David Suchet stars, with Anna Cropper and Bill Bailey(n)

programme.title: Sir Cliff Richard: 60 Years in Public and in Private
programme.desc: A profile of the singer filmed over a six-month period at his home in Portugal, at Abbey Road Studios and at Wimbledon, examining his career highs and lows and his record-breaking longevity. Cliff talks about the dark days that followed the police raid on his home in 2014, his battle to clear his name and the people whose support have got him through tough times. Featuring interviews with Barry Gibb, Olivia Newton-John, Hank Marvin, Jimmy Osmond, Andrew Lloyd Webber, Brian May and Joan Collins(n)

programme.title: The Halcyon
programme.desc: Drama set in 1940 at a five-star London hotel, showing the impact of the Second World War on families, politics, relationships and work across every social strata. The Halcyon's owner Lord Hamilton holds a secret meeting that attracts the interest of American guest Joe O'Hara, while manager Richard Garland must deal with the arrival of Lady Hamilton a day earlier than expected. Starring Olivia Williams, Steven Mackintosh, Alex Jennings and Matt Ryan(n)

programme.title: Only When I Laugh
programme.desc: The patients enjoy a good old moan about the standard of hospital radio - but it remains to be seen whether they can do any better(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar investigates a people smuggling ring, focusing his inquiries on the activities of a local yacht club. When it is revealed that one of the immigrants has gone on the run while suffering from smallpox, the Saint sets out to locate him before he can spread the disease(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: While an accused thief sits in jail waiting for his lawyer to arrange bail, Theo tries to decipher a phrase heard during an unsuccessful attempt to steal a paint company truck(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: A nine-year-old girl is shot dead in a feud between rival gangs, and Quincy finds himself caught up in an argument about how to deal with juvenile offenders(n)

Roger Moore directs and stars(n)
